pairs.hazard: Both propagate NULL (Doris live-probed = NULL; Trino prior = NULL). Safe re NULL, unlike DuckDB.
Both return NULL if any arg is NULL (Doris live-probed; Trino prior).
Both return NULL if any arg is NULL (Doris live-probed; Trino prior).
Out-of-bounds index -> NULL on both (Doris live-probed; Trino prior).
Both reverse by code point (Doris live-probed; Trino prior code-point-only). DuckDB is the grapheme-aware outlier.
Both replace ALL matches by default (Doris live-probed; Trino prior).

Doris ascii is byte-oriented (ascii('É')=195, first UTF-8 byte); Trino codepoint (requires varchar(1)). Doris diverges from BOTH Trino and DuckDB. Trino side from prior evidence, not re-run live — confirm with live Trino.
Doris strips ASCII space only; Trino (Java isWhitespace) strips tab/LF/CR/FF/VT. Different whitespace sets. Doris live; Trino prior.
Doris ASCII-space-only vs Trino Java-whitespace. Doris live; Trino prior.
Doris ASCII-space-only vs Trino Java-whitespace. Doris live; Trino prior.
Trino requires explicit bit-width arg; Doris infers. Doris live; Trino prior.
Trino float % uses IEEE-remainder-ish; Doris differs. Doris live; Trino prior.
Empty-match/array handling differs. Doris live; Trino prior.
Doris length()=bytes; Trino length()=characters. Doris live; Trino prior (char-based).
Out-of-domain acos(2): Doris returns NULL; Trino (JVM Math) returns NaN. In-domain agrees. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence + JVM domain semantics.
Out-of-domain asin(2): Doris returns NULL; Trino returns NaN. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
ln(0)/ln(-1): Doris returns NULL; Trino returns NaN (prior evidence 'NaN on x<=0'). In-domain agrees to last-ULP. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
sqrt(-1): Doris returns NULL; Trino returns NaN (prior evidence 'NaN on negative'). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
log2(0): Doris returns NULL; Trino returns NaN/domain (prior). In-domain agrees. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
log10(-1): Doris returns NULL; Trino returns NaN (prior). In-domain also differs by last-ULP (log10(3)). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris single-arg log(x) = natural log (log(10)=2.302585), not log10; two-arg log(b,x) agrees to last-ULP. Trino single-arg log semantics not re-probed this session — verify before mapping. Doris live.

In-domain agrees to ~15 sig digits but last-ULP differs (exp(0.5): DuckDB/Trino 1.6487212707001282 vs Doris 1.648721270700128). Safe under float-tolerant comparison. Doris live; Trino via live-DuckDB bridge (prior identical).
Last-ULP differs (cbrt(27): DuckDB/Trino 3.0000000000000004 vs Doris 3.0). Safe under float-tolerant comparison. Doris live; Trino via live-DuckDB bridge (prior identical).
Doris to_hex() returns NULL (to_hex(255)=NULL, to_hex('abc')=NULL); the working hex encoder is Doris hex(). Map Trino to_hex -> Doris hex(), never by name. Doris live.

Doris from_hex('616263') does NOT hex-decode (returns '363136323633'); the correct hex decoder is Doris unhex('616263')='abc' (matches Trino from_hex). Map Trino from_hex -> Doris unhex, never by name. Doris live.
Doris has no format_number function ('Can not found function format_number'). Doris live.

Doris url_encode('a b&c')='a+b%26c' (space -> '+'). Trino url_encode space-handling not re-probed this session (prior trino==duckdb 'identical' verdict did not test space; DuckDB uses %20) — verify Trino before mapping. Doris live.
Value semantics identical but Doris returns TINYINT 0/1 vs Trino BOOLEAN — boolean type mapping required (Doris renders BOOLEAN as 0/1 over MySQL protocol).
Doris sha1('abc') returns hex VARCHAR (matches DuckDB digest); Trino sha1 returns VARBINARY — unhex() wrap required for parity (same pattern as md5).
3-arg replace aligned; Trino 2-arg remove form replace(s,search) maps by passing '' as replacement. Doris live.
Doris has no levenshtein_distance; use Doris levenshtein (levenshtein('kitten','sitting')=3, matches). Rename only.
hamming_distance('abc','abd')=1 (Doris live; Trino same).
soundex('Robert')='R163' (Doris live; Trino same). (DuckDB needs splink_udfs; irrelevant to Trino->Doris.)
Pad, unicode pad, over-length truncation aligned.
repeat('x',0)='' both.
Extra from-chars deleted in both.
url_decode('a%20b')='a b' both.
NULL element skipped; NULL separator -> NULL in both.
substr('héllo',2,2)='él' (code-point aligned); Doris has substr.
avg{1,2,3,NULL}=2.0 (NULLs skipped); empty->NULL. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence (Trino==DuckDB).
sum{1,2,NULL}=3; empty->NULL both. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
count(x) skips NULL; count(*) counts rows. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
max ignores NULL. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
min ignores NULL. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Value semantics identical but Doris renders BOOLEAN as TINYINT 0/1 over MySQL protocol vs Trino true/false — boolean type mapping required. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Value semantics identical but Doris renders BOOLEAN as 0/1 vs Trino true/false — boolean type mapping. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Order-sensitive/nondeterministic arbitrary group value; matched this probe but not guaranteed. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
arg_max semantics matched (picks value at max key) but tie-breaking nondeterministic. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
arg_min semantics matched (min_by(y,x) picks y at min x = 'a') but tie-breaking nondeterministic. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Last-ULP float diff vs live DuckDB (0.9999008674099173 vs ...172) — value-equivalent. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence (Trino==DuckDB).
Last-ULP float diff — value-equivalent. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Last-ULP float diff (2.05 vs 2.049999999999999) — value-equivalent. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
stddev_pop{1,2,4}=1.247219128924647 exact match vs live DuckDB. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Last-ULP float diff — value-equivalent; sample (N-1) normalization matches. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Last-ULP float diff — value-equivalent. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Last-ULP float diff — value-equivalent; sample (N-1) matches. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Probe-verified live: DEFAULT MISMATCH. Trino stddev == stddev_samp (N-1) = 1.5275252316519468; Doris stddev == stddev_pop (N) = 1.247219128924647 over {1,2,4}. Doris's bare stddev is POPULATION, not sample. Must map Trino stddev -> Doris stddev_samp for parity, never by name.

Probe-verified live: DEFAULT MISMATCH. Trino variance == var_samp (N-1) = 2.3333333333333335; Doris variance == var_pop (N) = 1.555555555555556 over {1,2,4}. Doris's bare variance is POPULATION, not sample. Must map Trino variance -> Doris var_samp for parity, never by name.

Probe-verified live: different formula/default. Over {1,2,4,8}: DuckDB(=Trino sample) 1.1376243669576889 vs Doris 0.6568077344996993 (population moment). Sample-vs-population divergence. Doris live; Trino via DuckDB bridge.

Probe-verified live: different formula/default. Over {1,2,4,8}: DuckDB(=Trino sample excess) 0.7576559546313808 vs Doris -1.098979206049149 (population excess). Sign and magnitude differ. Doris live; Trino via DuckDB bridge.
Last-ULP float diff — value-equivalent. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Last-ULP float diff (2.05 vs 2.049999999999999) — value-equivalent. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Returns a LIST/ARRAY (type layer). Order is input-order-dependent and nondeterministic without ORDER BY: DuckDB preserved input order [3,1,2] while Doris returned [1,2,3] on the same UNION-ALL. Add explicit ORDER BY for deterministic parity. Doris live; Trino via DuckDB bridge.

Returns a MAP (type layer). Doris has map_agg; Trino map_agg builds key->value. Element ordering within the map is nondeterministic — conditionally-equivalent. DuckDB lacks map_agg (Trino-only bridge; compare via histogram/map). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Probe-verified live: incompatible return type/semantics. Trino histogram returns a MAP(value->count) (matching DuckDB {a=2,b=1}); Doris histogram returns an approximate-quantile bucket JSON string. Not interchangeable by name. Doris live; Trino via DuckDB bridge.
row_number() OVER(ORDER BY x) = 1,2,3 both. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
rank() with tie = 1,1,3 (gap). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
dense_rank() with tie = 1,1,2 (no gap). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
ntile(2) over 3 rows = 1,1,2 both. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
lag(x) OVER(ORDER BY x) -> NULL,1,2 both. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
lead(x) OVER(ORDER BY x) -> 2,3,NULL both. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
first_value(x) OVER(ORDER BY x) = 1,1,1 both. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
last_value(x) OVER(ORDER BY x) = 1,2,3 both — same default frame (running window). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
nth_value(x,2) OVER(ORDER BY x) = NULL,2,2 both. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Values identical (0,0.5,1) but Doris renders integral doubles as '0'/'1' vs '0.0'/'1.0' — numeric rendering only. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Values identical (0.333..,0.666..,1) but Doris renders trailing 1.0 as '1' — numeric rendering only. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

SIGNATURE MISMATCH: Trino date_add(VARCHAR unit, BIGINT n, timestamp) (e.g. date_add('day',1,ts)); Doris date_add(date, INTERVAL n unit) / date_add(date, n). Different arg order and arity — must remap unit-string form to INTERVAL, never push by name. (Prior trino-duckdb corpus also flags date_add same-name/incompatible-signature.) Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

BOTH use MySQL-style '%'-specifiers (NOT Joda): Trino date_format(ts,'%Y-%m-%d')='2024-01-02' and Doris date_format(TIMESTAMP '2024-01-02 03:04:05','%Y-%m-%d')='2024-01-02' (probe-verified live on Doris; Trino from prior evidence). %H:%i:%s and %M(month name) align. NOTE the family trap: Trino format_datetime/parse_datetime use Joda 'yyyy' — those do NOT map to Doris date_format. Doris also partially tolerates 'yyyy-MM-dd' but bare 'yyyy' is a literal, so map ONLY via '%' specs. Conditionally-equivalent on the '%'-spec dialect.

Values align for the intersection unit set (day..year): date_trunc('month',ts)='2024-02-01 00:00:00', 'quarter'->'2024-01-01' both. Trino returns TIMESTAMP; Doris DATETIME (Doris renders no fractional part, Trino/DuckDB '.0'). Week unit Monday-start on both. Conditionally-equivalent, verify unit set. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
date('2024-02-29')='2024-02-29' — DuckDB bridge SAME as Doris; Trino date(x) cast-to-date equivalent. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
day(DATE '2024-02-29')=29 (DuckDB bridge==Doris). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence (prior corpus: year/month/day aligned).
month(DATE '2024-02-29')=2 (bridge==Doris). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
year(DATE '2024-02-29')=2024 (bridge==Doris). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
quarter(DATE '2024-02-29')=1 (bridge==Doris). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

WEEK-MODE MISMATCH: Trino week/week_of_year is ISO-8601 (Mon-start, week 01 contains Jan 4) so week('2023-01-01')=52; Doris default week() is mode 0 (Sunday-start) -> week('2023-01-01')=1, week('2024-01-01')=0. Prior trino-duckdb corpus confirms Trino week is ISO==DuckDB(52). Divergent unless Doris week mode pinned to ISO (weekofyear or week(d,3)). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
minute(TIMESTAMP '2024-01-02 03:04:05')=4 (bridge==Doris). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
second(...)=5 (bridge==Doris). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
hour(...)=3 (bridge==Doris). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

NUMBERING MISMATCH: Trino day_of_week/dow is ISO 1..7 Monday=1..Sunday=7; Doris dayofweek is 1..7 Sunday=1..Saturday=7 (MySQL). Sunday '2024-01-07': Trino dow=7, Doris dayofweek=1. Monday '2024-01-08': Trino=1, Doris=2. Off by rotation — divergent. (Prior corpus: Trino dow 1..7 Mon=1 vs DuckDB 0..6 Sun=0.) Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
day_of_year/doy: leap-day '2024-02-29'=60 (bridge==Doris; prior corpus Trino doy==DuckDB). Rename only. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

WEEK-YEAR MODE MISMATCH: Trino year_of_week/yow is ISO week-year: yow('2023-01-01')=2022; Doris yearweek('2023-01-01')=202301 (default mode 0, embeds week too and picks year 2023). Different value AND encoding (Doris packs YYYYWW). To get the ISO year-of-week from Doris use a mode-3 yearweek/2 and strip week. Divergent. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Trino year_of_week returns the ISO week-year (2022 for '2023-01-01'). Doris has NO year_of_week function; its yearweek packs YYYYWW under a different (mode-0) week definition. No direct name equivalent — express via Doris yearweek(d,3) DIV 100. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

from_unixtime(0)='1970-01-01 00:00:00' on Doris (probe-verified live); Trino from_unixtime(double) returns TIMESTAMP(3) WITH TIME ZONE in the session zone (prior corpus: same absolute instant, rendering session-zone dependent). SESSION-ZONE dependent — the harness cannot pre-SET zone, so equivalence holds only for the same session zone. Doris also has a 2-arg from_unixtime(sec,fmt) (from_unixtime(0,'yyyy-MM-dd')='1970-01-01'). Conditionally-equivalent. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
from_iso8601_date('2024-02-29')='2024-02-29' on Doris (probe-verified live); Trino from_iso8601_date parses an ISO date to DATE identically. Both leap-day safe. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

to_iso8601(DATE '2024-02-29')='2024-02-29' both (aligned). For TIMESTAMP: Doris to_iso8601(TIMESTAMP '2024-02-29 03:04:05')='2024-02-29T03:04:05.000000' (6 fractional digits, no zone); Trino to_iso8601(timestamp) yields '...T03:04:05.000' (3 digits) and for tz-aware types appends the offset. Fractional-precision/zone rendering differs — conditionally-equivalent. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Both return current instant; Trino now() is TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE (session zone, ISO render); Doris now()='2026-07-13 07:38:31' (space sep, second precision, session-zone). SESSION-ZONE dependent — cannot pre-SET in harness. Value-equivalent instant, format/precision/zone differ. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

current_date rendered identically live ('2026-07-13'); Trino current_date is date in session zone. SESSION-ZONE dependent — equivalence only for matching session zone. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Rename: Trino last_day_of_month(DATE '2024-02-15')='2024-02-29'; Doris last_day(DATE '2024-02-15')='2024-02-29' (probe-verified live, leap-Feb). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: array_distinct([1,1,2,3,3])=[1,2,3] (size 3, e1=1 — preserves first-occurrence order). Trino array_distinct also preserves order (==DuckDB list_distinct set-wise, but DuckDB returns reversed [3,2,1]). Set/length identical; ORDER matches Trino but the DuckDB bridge reorders — order-preservation must be verified per element, not by raw render. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

SIGNATURE trap. Doris live: array_first is a LAMBDA/predicate fn — array_first(x->x>0,[10,20,30])=10 (first element matching the predicate; the array is the SECOND arg). Trino array_first(array) returns the plain first element with no predicate. Arity/semantics differ -> map by rewriting to array_first(x->true, arr) or element_at(arr,1). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

SIGNATURE trap (same as array_first). Doris live: array_last(x->x>0,[10,20,30])=30 — lambda predicate first, array second. Trino array_last(array) is the plain last element. Map via array_last(x->true,arr) or element_at(arr,-1). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Doris live: array_intersect([1,2,3],[2,3,4])=[2,3] (e1=2, size 2). Matches Trino (prior corpus: Trino array_intersect==DuckDB list_intersect identical). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: array_position([10,20,30],20)=2 (1-based), and NOT-FOUND array_position([10,20,30],99)=0. Trino array_position is also 1-based and returns 0 when absent (matches). NOTE the DuckDB bridge differs on not-found (list_position returns NULL, not 0) — do not adjudicate via DuckDB here. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: array_sort([3,1,2]) ascending -> e1=1,e3=3; NULL-bearing array_sort([3,NULL,1]) keeps NULL (size preserved =3). Trino array_sort places NULLs last; verify NULL ordering per element before relying on it. Ascending default aligns. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: array_except([1,2,3,4],[2,4])=[1,3] (size 2, e1=1). Trino array_except is the same set-difference. (Prior corpus marked it no-equivalent on the DuckDB side — DuckDB lacks it — but Doris HAS it and matches Trino.) Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: array_remove([1,2,3,2],2)=[1,3] (size 2, e1=1) — removes ALL occurrences. Trino array_remove is the same. (Prior corpus: DuckDB lacks it, needed list_filter workaround; Doris HAS it and matches Trino.) Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: array_union([1,2,3],[3,4]) -> size 4 (distinct union). Trino array_union is the same. (Prior corpus: DuckDB needed list_distinct(list_concat(...)); Doris HAS array_union by name and matches Trino.) Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: arrays_overlap([1,2],[2,3])=1 (overlap) and arrays_overlap([1,2],[3,4])=0 (disjoint). Value semantics match Trino, but Doris returns TINYINT 0/1 while Trino returns BOOLEAN — boolean type mapping required (Doris renders BOOLEAN as 0/1 over the MySQL protocol). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: array length is array_size([1,2,3])=3; Doris cardinality also works on arrays, but the portable name is array_size (Doris has NO array_length). Trino cardinality(array) returns element count -> value matches via array_size, but the name maps to array_size (or element count). Map-cardinality (Trino) also -> not covered here. Rename required. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: element_at([10,20,30],2)=20 (1-based) but OUT-OF-BOUNDS element_at([10,20,30],9)=NULL (returns NULL, does not throw). Trino element_at on an array THROWS on out-of-bounds index (does NOT return NULL) — divergent on the OOB path. In-bounds 1-based access aligns. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: 'contains' does NOT exist ('Can not found function contains'); the array-membership fn is array_contains([1,2,3],2) -> 1 (TINYINT). Trino contains(array,elem) returns BOOLEAN true. Both a NAME map (contains->array_contains) AND a boolean 0/1-vs-true/false type map are required. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: array_join([1,2,3],'-')='1-2-3'; with a NULL element array_join([1,NULL,3],'-')='1-3' (NULL SKIPPED when no 3rd nullReplacement arg). Trino array_join(x,sep) ALSO skips NULLs by default, but the 3-arg form array_join(x,sep,nullStr) semantics/prior corpus marks array_join->DuckDB array_to_string as divergent (DuckDB array_to_string has no null-replacement 3rd arg). Value of the 2-arg NULL-skip aligns Doris<->Trino; adjudicated divergent to carry the 3-arg/DuckDB-bridge hazard forward. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

NULL-propagation divergence. Doris live: array_max([1,2,3])=3 and array_max([1,NULL,2])=2 (Doris SKIPS NULL elements). Trino array_max PROPAGATES NULL — array_max(array[1,null,2]) returns NULL if any element is NULL. So on a NULL-bearing array Doris(2) != Trino(NULL). Non-NULL arrays align. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

NULL-propagation divergence (same as array_max). Doris live: array_min([3,1,2])=1 and array_min([1,NULL,2])=1 (Doris SKIPS NULL). Trino array_min propagates NULL -> NULL when any element is NULL. Non-NULL arrays align; NULL-bearing arrays diverge. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: split_by_string('a,b,c',',') -> size 3 works; Doris has split_by_string (and split_part), NOT a Trino-style split(str,delim,limit). Prior corpus already marks Trino split->DuckDB string_split as divergent (Trino split has a 3-arg limit form and empty-delimiter behavior differs). Map Trino split -> Doris split_by_string, never by name; verify empty-delimiter and limit-arg per case. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: shuffle([1,2,3]) returns a size-3 array (nondeterministic permutation). Trino shuffle is likewise a random permutation. Equal as a multiset but element ORDER is nondeterministic per call -> conditionally-equivalent (nondeterministic; compare as multiset only). (Prior corpus: DuckDB has no shuffle -> no-equivalent on the DuckDB side; Doris HAS shuffle.) Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: sequence(1,5) -> size 5, last element =5 (INCLUSIVE stop). Trino sequence(1,5) is also inclusive [1..5]. (DuckDB bridge: generate_series is inclusive/range is exclusive — use generate_series, per prior corpus conditionally-equivalent.) Doris matches Trino inclusive semantics. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Doris live: cosine_similarity([1,2,3],[1,2,3])=1.0. Matches Trino/DuckDB (list_cosine_similarity=1.0) on the value; float representation/ULP may differ. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live: cosine_distance([1,2,3],[4,5,6])=0.02536809 vs DuckDB/Trino list_cosine_distance=0.025368153802923787 — same value, differs only in the last ULP digits / render precision (Doris ~8 sig figs). Float ULP diff -> conditionally-equivalent. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Map cardinality maps by name: Doris cardinality(map) and Trino cardinality(map) both return the entry count (matches DuckDB cardinality(MAP)=2). Only the ARRAY overload of cardinality needs the array_size rename (see the cardinality/array_size pair). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris live == DuckDB live (json_extract('{"a":"x"}','$.a')='"x"' with QUOTES retained; $.b='[10,20]'; $.b[0]='10'; nested $.c.d='"x"'; missing path NULL). Prior corpus (trino-duckdb-hazards.json) records Trino json_extract == DuckDB json_extract (conditionally-equivalent, both return a JSON value with quotes retained). Chaining: Trino==DuckDB==Doris live -> identical. Trino json_extract_scalar (unquoted) maps to Doris json_extract_string. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Doris json_parse probe-verified live: json_parse('[1,2,3]')='[1,2,3]' and json_parse('{"a":1}')='{"a":1}' (VARCHAR text -> JSON value), matching Trino json_parse's happy-path role. DIVERGENCE in error handling: Trino json_parse THROWS on malformed input; Doris json_parse('not json')='null' (returns JSON null silently, does NOT error). Value-equivalent on valid JSON, differs on invalid input (Doris swallows the error). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Trino json_format(JSON)->VARCHAR (serialize a JSON value to its text form). No Doris function by that name (DuckDB also lacks it — prior corpus marks Trino json_format as no-equivalent). Doris achieves the same via CAST(json AS string) / to-string rendering, but there is no named json_format built-in. Prior corpus: no-equivalent. Doris live inventory checked; Trino from prior evidence.

RE-ADJUDICATED vs Doris (prior corpus said no-equivalent because DuckDB has no e()). Doris HAS e(): e()=2.718281828459045, exactly Trino's e() (Euler's number). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence. Identical.

RE-ADJUDICATED vs Doris (prior corpus no-equivalent vs DuckDB). Doris crc32('abc')=891568578 which equals the standard CRC-32 of 'abc' (zlib.crc32('abc')=891568578), matching Trino's crc32(varbinary) numeric result. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence. Identical (Trino takes varbinary, Doris varchar — same underlying bytes for ASCII).

RE-ADJUDICATED vs Doris (prior no-equivalent vs DuckDB). Doris normal_cdf(0,1,0.5)=0.6914624612740131 = 0.5*(1+erf(0.5/sqrt(2))) = Phi(0.5), and the arg order (mean, sd, value) matches Trino's normal_cdf(mean, sd, value). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence. Identical.

RE-ADJUDICATED vs Doris (prior no-equivalent vs DuckDB — DuckDB lacks it). Doris regexp_count('a1b2c3','[0-9]')=3, matching Trino's regexp_count(string, pattern) count of non-overlapping matches. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence. Identical.

Doris live via bridge (Trino==DuckDB prior). Doris regexp_extract REQUIRES a group index: regexp_extract('abc123','([0-9]+)',0)='123' (whole match, group 0), ('...',1)='123', and ('([a-z]+)([0-9]+)',2)='123' — group semantics match Trino. But Trino's 2-arg regexp_extract(str, pattern) (whole match) has NO direct Doris arity — Doris errors on the 2-arg form. Conditionally-equivalent: use the explicit-group 3-arg form on Doris.

RE-ADJUDICATED vs Doris. Doris truncate(3.14159,2)=3.14 matches Trino truncate(x,n) (truncate toward zero to n decimals). Prior corpus already marked truncate identical for Trino==DuckDB rename; confirmed live on Doris. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence. Identical.

RE-ADJUDICATED vs Doris (prior no-equivalent vs DuckDB 'different shape'). Doris width_bucket(5.0,0.0,10.0,5)=3, exactly Trino's width_bucket(operand, bound1, bound2, bucketCount): 5 buckets over (0,10] width 2, 5.0 falls in (4,6]=bucket 3. Signature and result match. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence. Identical.

RE-ADJUDICATED vs Doris (prior no-equivalent vs DuckDB). Doris parse_data_size('1kB')=1024, matching Trino parse_data_size (kB=1024 bytes, decimal result). Doris live; Trino from prior evidence. Identical for the value; Trino returns decimal(38,0), Doris returns the integer 1024 (same magnitude).

Non-deterministic RNG. Doris has random()/rand() returning double in [0,1) (probe-verified [0,1)). Trino rand()/random() same distribution; bounded Trino forms random(n)/random(m,n) also exist. Never pushable/comparable value-for-value. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Non-deterministic. Doris random() returns double in [0,1) (probe-verified). Trino random() same; Trino also has bounded random(n) (0..n) and random(m,n) integer forms whose exact bound semantics were not probed on Doris. Never pushable. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
Both produce a canonical 36-char UUID string (Doris length=36). Non-deterministic — same shape, never pushable/comparable value-for-value. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

CONSTRUCTOR SIGNATURE differs. Trino map(array(K),array(V)) takes two parallel arrays (like DuckDB; Trino==DuckDB prior). Doris map(k1,v1,k2,v2) takes flattened alternating scalars. Probe-verified: Doris does not build the same map from the two-array form (map_keys(map([1,2],['a','b']))=[[1, 2]] on Doris vs [1,2] expected). Must translate the constructor. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Same semantics once the map is built with each engine's own constructor. Doris map_keys(map(1,'a',2,'b'))='[1, 2]' matches Trino/DuckDB key set; array_size=2. Conditionally-equivalent due to the divergent map() constructor and array render. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Same value set; Doris map_values(map(1,'a',2,'b'))='["a", "b"]' (JSON-quoted render) matches Trino/DuckDB values element-wise. Depends on divergent map() constructor and render. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.

Element type: Trino map_entries returns array(row(K,V)); Doris returns array of struct with fields key/value: map_entries(map(1,'a',2,'b'))='[{"key":1, "value":"a"}, {"key":2, "value":"b"}]'. Compatible shape (unnamed row vs named key/value struct) — needs a type-layer mapping, plus the divergent map() constructor. Doris live; Trino from prior evidence.
table function (row-generating), not scalar-probeable through the shared-expr harness; needs a table-function harness for a row-set/ordering comparison.
